county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Excellent preservation — state capital county.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1771 from Cumberland, Johnston, and Orange counties. Raleigh became state capital 1792. Parent of Durham (partly, 1881). NC State Archives physically located in Wake County — the richest genealogical repository in the state. Wake County is NC's most populous county.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with Wake County ROD online — excellent portal from 1785. FamilySearch has extensive collections. Olivia Raney Local History Library (Wake County Public Library) is outstanding. NC State Archives in Raleigh is the definitive research facility for all NC genealogy. Wake County Genealogical Society very active.

source_profiles.source_name: Wake County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Excellent online deed search with images. One of NC's best ROD portals.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1781 and registers from 1851.
Outstanding local research facility. Genealogical collections, vertical files, cemetery records, family histories, and published abstracts for Wake County.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Good preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1779 when extinct Bute County was divided — Warren (northern part) and Franklin (southern part). Pre-1779 records in Bute County papers; pre-1764 in Granville County. Parent of Vance (partly, 1881). Wealthy antebellum plantation county — significant African American genealogy resources.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — deeds from 1779. Warren County ROD has online portal. For pre-1779, check Bute County records at NC State Archives and Granville County records. Warren County Memorial Library has local history. Significant plantation records for African American research.

source_profiles.source_name: Warren County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts. Significant for antebellum research — detailed slave inventories.
Marriage bonds from 1779 and registers from 1851.

source_profiles.source_name: NC Estate Files — Warren County Index
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Free online estate file index. Particularly rich for this plantation county.

county_metadata.record_losses: Courthouse burned during Civil War 1864 — Battle of Plymouth. Confederate forces under Hoke recaptured Plymouth and the CSS Albemarle engagement. Many records destroyed.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Tyrrell 1799. Pre-1799 records in Tyrrell County (deeds from 1735). Civil War destruction significant — Plymouth changed hands multiple times 1862-1864.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch for surviving records. Washington County ROD has limited online portal. NC State Archives critical for burned county research. Pre-1799 records in Tyrrell County. Census records from 1800 complete. Adjacent county records may fill gaps.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Digitized microfilm. Surviving deed records — significant gaps from 1864 fire.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Many pre-1864 deeds destroyed in Civil War courthouse burning.

source_profiles.source_name: NC State Archives — Washington County Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State Archives holdings critical for this burned county. May contain materials deposited before the 1864 fire.
Marriage registers from 1851. Pre-1864 bonds largely lost in fire.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1849 from Ashe, Caldwell, Wilkes, and Yancey counties. Pre-1849 records in all four parent counties. Appalachian State University in Boone. Named for the Watauga Settlement (1769) — one of the earliest trans-Appalachian settlements. Daniel Boone explored this area.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Watauga County ROD online. For pre-1849, check Ashe, Wilkes, and Yancey county records. Appalachian State University W.L. Eury Appalachian Collection has significant mountain heritage materials. Watauga County Historical Society is active.

source_profiles.source_name: Watauga County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: W.L. Eury Appalachian Collection — Appalachian State University
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Significant mountain heritage materials. Oral histories, family papers, and Appalachian genealogical resources covering Watauga and surrounding mountain counties.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Dobbs County (extinct) 1779. Dobbs was formed from Johnston 1758. When Dobbs was dissolved 1791, it became Wayne and Lenoir. Pre-1779 records in Dobbs County papers. Goldsboro was a major Civil War-era railroad junction. Seymour Johnson Air Force Base (1942).
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — deeds from 1779. Wayne County ROD has online portal. For pre-1779, search Dobbs County records at NC State Archives and Johnston County records. Wayne County Public Library has local history room.

source_profiles.source_name: Wayne County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1779 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.record_losses: No known courthouse record losses. Good preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Surry 1778 and the former district of Washington. Pre-1778 records in Surry County (formed from Rowan 1771). Parent of Ashe (1799), Watauga (partly, 1849), Caldwell (partly, 1841), Alexander (partly, 1847). Tom Dooley murder (1866) occurred here — became folk song 'Tom Dula.' Wilkes was a hotbed of both Tory and Patriot activity during the Revolution.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch — deeds from 1778. Wilkes County ROD has online portal. Wilkes Heritage Museum in Wilkesboro has genealogical collections. For pre-1778, use Surry and Rowan county records.

source_profiles.source_name: Wilkes County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills, inventories, and estate accounts from county formation.
Marriage bonds from 1778 and registers from 1851.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed 1855 from Edgecombe, Nash, Johnston, and Wayne counties. Pre-1855 records scattered across all four parent counties. Wilson became a major tobacco market in late 19th century.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Wilson County ROD online. For pre-1855, primarily check Edgecombe County (excellent colonial records from 1732) and Nash County records. Wilson County Public Library has local history room.

source_profiles.source_name: Wilson County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Surry 1850. Pre-1850 records in Surry County (formed from Rowan 1771). Named for the Yadkin River. Predominantly agricultural county. Yadkin Valley wine region.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Yadkin County ROD online. For pre-1850, use Surry County records (no losses). Yadkin County Historical Society has local collections.

source_profiles.source_name: Yadkin County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Formed from Buncombe and Burke 1833. Pre-1833 records in both parent counties. Parent of Mitchell (1861), Madison (partly, 1851), Watauga (partly, 1849). Mount Mitchell (6,684 ft) — highest point east of the Mississippi — is in Yancey County. Named for Bartlett Yancey, NC state legislator.
county_metadata.research_tips: Start with FamilySearch and Yancey County ROD online. For pre-1833, check Buncombe County (burned 1830/1865 — difficult) and Burke County (severely burned 1865). Census records from 1840. Yancey County Historical and Genealogical Society is active. Mountain Gateway Museum has local history.

source_profiles.source_name: Yancey County Register of Deeds Online
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Online deed search from county formation.
Wills and estate records from county formation.
Marriage bonds and registers from county formation.
